title: CDE Boundary Traffic — Unexpected Corporate-to-Payment Flows

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI DSS 1.3.1 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI DSS 1.3.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.11.3: CDE Boundary Traffic — Unexpected Corporate-to-Payment Flows.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.11.3' running on sourcetype pan:traffic and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
spl:
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:traffic" action=allowed earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_corp_zones.csv zone AS src_zone OUTPUT corp_zone
| lookup pci_payment_subnets.csv cidr AS dest OUTPUT payment_tier
| where corp_zone="true" AND payment_tier="card_processing"
| stats count, values(dest_port) as ports, values(app) as apps by src, dest, rule
| where count>5
| sort - count
description: Highlights allowed sessions from general corporate segments into payment subnets so segmentation and NSC rules can be validated between assessments, not only during annual penetration tests.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PCI DSS compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes netfw and sourcetypes pan:traffic. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Populate CIDR lookups from network diagrams approved for PCI. (2) Whitelist jump-host rules via `where NOT match(rule,"(?i)bastion|jump")`. (3) Feed hits to the segmentation validation runbook. (4) Map findings to Req 11 segmentation testing follow-up. Indexes required: `index=netfw`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=pan:traffic`.
visualization: Heatmap (src_zone x dest_zone counts), table (top flows), Sankey optional for zone paths.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: Firewall changes during scheduled change windows, controlled segmentation rollouts, or planned audits.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=netfw` `sourcetype="pan:traffic"` — `src`, `dest`, `src_zone`, `dest_zone`, `action`, `rule`; lookups `pci_corp_zones.csv`, `pci_payment_subnets.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Populate CIDR lookups from network diagrams approved for PCI. (2) Whitelist jump-host rules via `where NOT match(rule,"(?i)bastion|jump")`. (3) Feed hits to the segmentation validation runbook. (4) Map findings to Req 11 segmentation testing follow-up.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:traffic" action=allowed earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_corp_zones.csv zone AS src_zone OUTPUT corp_zone
| lookup pci_payment_subnets.csv cidr AS dest OUTPUT payment_tier
| where corp_zone="true" AND payment_tier="card_processing"
| stats count, values(dest_port) as ports, values(app) as apps by src, dest, rule
| where count>5
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**CDE Boundary Traffic — Unexpected Corporate-to-Payment Flows (PCI DSS Req 1.2.3, 1.3.1)** — Highlights allowed sessions from general corporate segments into payment subnets so segmentation and NSC rules can be validated between assessments, not only during annual penetration tests.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=netfw` `sourcetype="pan:traffic"` — `src`, `dest`, `src_zone`, `dest_zone`, `action`, `rule`; lookups `pci_corp_zones.csv`, `pci_payment_subnets.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: netfw; **sourcetype**: pan:traffic. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=netfw, sourcetype="pan:traffic", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where corp_zone="true" AND payment_tier="card_processing"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by src, dest, rule** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where count>5` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**CDE Boundary Traffic — Unexpected Corporate-to-Payment Flows (PCI DSS Req 1.2.3, 1.3.1)** — Highlights allowed sessions from general corporate segments into payment subnets so segmentation and NSC rules can be validated between assessments, not only during annual penetration tests.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=netfw` `sourcetype="pan:traffic"` — `src`, `dest`, `src_zone`, `dest_zone`, `action`, `rule`; lookups `pci_corp_zones.csv`, `pci_payment_subnets.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the cardholder data audit log, validate a few expected payment-ops and assessor activities, and confirm events, fields, and index permissions are correct.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap (src_zone x dest_zone counts), table (top flows), Sankey optional for zone paths.
grandmaExplanation: We highlights allowed sessions from general corporate segments into payment subnets so segmentation and NSC rules can be validated between assessments, not only during annual penetration tests. PCI DSS is about protecting card and payment data when we run these checks.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for Change (CIM data model), All_Changes dataset.
